name: Personal Expense Tracker
prompt: Build a complete personal expense tracker as a React + Vite application. Implement every requirement below in the delivered product; do not defer, mock, or describe any listed capability instead of implementing it. Preserve every REQ-* identifier in each gate's governance artifacts for G0, G1, G2, and G3 so requirement coverage remains traceable. The project's package.json and package-lock.json are FIXED and already declare every dependency and the build/test scripts you need (React, Vite, Vitest, jsdom, @testing-library/*); do NOT modify them or add dependencies -- the build sandbox is OFFLINE and installs nothing new, so build only with the provided libraries plus the standard browser DOM and localStorage APIs. The product is graded in a real browser: persist state with the real localStorage API (not in-memory or session-only state) so it survives a full page reload, and give every interactive control a visible keyboard-focus indicator (a real focus outline; never `outline: none` without a visible replacement). [REQ-FIELDS] Each expense must have an editable description, amount, category, and date before it is added. [REQ-ADD] A user must be able to add an expense and see it in the expense list immediately. [REQ-DELETE] A user must be able to delete a specific expense without deleting other expenses, and that deletion must remain in effect after a full page refresh. [REQ-RECONCILE] A user must be able to mark a specific expense as reconciled, see a clear reconciled state, and retain that state after a full page refresh without changing other expenses. [REQ-FILTER] A user must be able to filter expenses by category, including Food and Travel, and return to an All view. [REQ-PERSIST] Added expenses, deleted expenses, and reconciled state must persist after a full page refresh. [REQ-ACCESSIBLE] Provide an accessible, keyboard-usable interface with associated labels, meaningful control names, visible focus, and status that is not communicated by color alone.

requirements.description: Each new expense captures description, amount, category, and date.
The user can add an expense and immediately see it in the list.
The user can delete one expense without affecting another, and deletion survives refresh.
The user can visibly mark one expense reconciled without affecting another, and the state survives refresh.
The user can filter by category, including Food and Travel, and restore the All view.
Added, deleted, and reconciled state remains correct after a full page refresh.
The interface is keyboard usable and exposes labels, meaningful control names, visible focus, and non-color-only status.
